item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll inspect the three source modules, make the schema/model/query changes, then run the test suite against the live database.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs"
/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"preferred|CREATE TABLE accounts|accounts \\(\" src && rg --files src"
/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"preferred|CREATE TABLE accounts|accounts \\(\" src && rg --files src"

item.text: The field is now persisted as an optional constrained column, returned by the account model, and selected into order summaries. I’m running the benchmark tests now.

item.text:
Implemented `preferredPaymentMethod` across schema, account create/update/retrieval, and `getOrderSummary`.

Also aligned live-schema defaults/constraints needed for the existing acceptance setup.

Verification: `npm test` passes.
